Skip to contents
razer 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Package index

Population frame

Struct-of-arrays data store for agents and patches, and the epidemic state codes used by the model kernels.

LaserFrame
Fixed-capacity struct-of-arrays population or patch data store.
laser_states()
Named integer vector of epidemic compartment state codes.

Distributions

Parameterized probability distributions used to draw state-timer durations (e.g. incubation and infectious periods).

Distribution
A parameterized probability distribution that can be sampled repeatedly.
dist_beta()
Create a beta distribution on the open interval (0, 1).
dist_constant()
Create a degenerate (constant) distribution that always returns value.
dist_exp()
Create an exponential distribution with rate rate.
dist_gamma()
Create a gamma distribution parameterized by shape and scale.
dist_logistic()
Create a logistic distribution with the given location and scale.
dist_lognormal()
Create a log-normal distribution.
dist_normal()
Create a normal (Gaussian) distribution.
dist_poisson()
Create a Poisson distribution with rate (mean) lambda.
dist_uniform()
Create a continuous uniform distribution on the half-open interval [low, high).

Model step kernels

Per-tick transition kernels for composing SI / SIR / SEIR / SEIRS-family models. Call them downstream-first within a tick — see “Composing models: order of per-tick update operations” below.

model-composition update-order
Composing models: order of per-tick update operations
step_births_cbr()
Stochastic birth step using a crude birth rate.
step_exposed_ei()
Timer-based E→I transition (SEIR kernel).
step_infectious_ir()
Timer-based I→R transition (SIR / SEIR / SEIRS kernel).
step_infectious_is()
Timer-based I→S transition for SIS models (no immunity).
step_mortality_cdr()
Stochastic mortality step using a crude death rate.
step_recovered_rs()
Timer-based R→S transition for waning-immunity models.
step_transmission_se()
Stochastic S→E exposure step (SEIR kernel).
step_transmission_si()
Stochastic S→I transmission step (SI / SIR kernel).
